summary: Terminal markdown renderer used by doc-facing seams such as `narrative` and `quality` for width-specific rendered review artifacts.

commands: if command -v brew >/dev/null 2>&1; then brew install glow; else GOPATH="$(go env GOPATH)" && mkdir -p "$GOPATH/bin" "$HOME/.local/bin" && go install github.com/charmbracelet/glow/v2@latest && ln -sf "$GOPATH/bin/glow" "$HOME/.local/bin/glow"; fi
notes: Preferred install paths are Homebrew or `go install`.
The repo-managed install path uses Homebrew when available, otherwise `go install` plus a `~/.local/bin/glow` symlink so doctor can find the binary on common Linux setups.
Use the same mechanism that originally installed the binary. Homebrew installs can use `brew upgrade glow`; Go installs can rerun `go install github.com/charmbracelet/glow/v2@latest`.

detect.failure_hint: Install `glow` so markdown preview seams can produce real rendered artifacts instead of degraded source snapshots.

healthcheck.failure_hint: The `glow` binary exists but does not respond cleanly. Reinstall it from a clean upstream release or rerun the repo-managed install path.

version_expectation.constraint: Prefer a recent upstream-supported glow release.

notes: Upstream documents Homebrew as `brew install glow`.

when_missing: Markdown preview seams degrade to source-copy artifacts that explicitly say rendered review did not happen.
Do not claim rendered readability proof for README, landing docs, or specs without `glow`.
when_version_mismatch: Treat layout or width-render drift as a manual review point before trusting generated preview artifacts.

host_notes: This binary backs the repo-owned `markdown-preview` support seam instead of replacing it.
Go installs may be symlinked into `~/.local/bin` so the binary is both PATH-visible and still traceable back to the Go install root.
